summary: Iteration 48 Testing Complete - SLA Management Feature. Backend: 20/20 tests passed (CRUD operations, filtering, auth). Frontend: All UI components verified working - page loads with 6 seeded rules, department filter tabs work, stats cards display correct counts, rule cards expand to show escalation table, toggle switch activates/deactivates rules with toast notifications, create modal opens with all form fields, delete confirmation dialog works.
passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/sla/rules returns all 6 seeded rules
Backend: GET /api/sla/rules verifies correct rule structure (id, name, department, applies_to, is_active, levels)
Backend: GET /api/sla/rules?department=Sales filters correctly (3 Sales rules)
Backend: GET /api/sla/rules?department=Customer Service filters correctly (1 CS rule)
Backend: GET /api/sla/rules?department=HR filters correctly (2 HR rules)
Backend: POST /api/sla/rules creates new rule successfully
Backend: POST /api/sla/rules verify persistence via GET
Backend: PUT /api/sla/rules/{id} updates rule successfully
Backend: PUT /api/sla/rules/{id} verify update persistence
Backend: PATCH /api/sla/rules/{id}/toggle toggles to inactive
Backend: PATCH /api/sla/rules/{id}/toggle toggles back to active
Backend: DELETE /api/sla/rules/{id} deletes rule
Backend: DELETE /api/sla/rules/{id} verify removal
Backend: PUT non-existent rule returns 404
Backend: PATCH toggle non-existent rule returns 404
Backend: DELETE non-existent rule returns 404
Backend: Seeded Sales rules verified (New Lead First Contact, Inactive Lead Warning, Pipeline Stale Lead)
Backend: Seeded CS rules verified (CS Activation Call)
Backend: Seeded HR rules verified (HR Leave Approval, HR Regularization Approval)
Backend: GET /api/sla/rules requires authentication (returns 401/403)
Frontend: SLA Management page loads at /sla-management with data-testid='sla-management-page'
Frontend: Page title shows 'SLA Management'
Frontend: Stats cards show Total Rules (6), Active (6), Inactive (0), Departments (3)
Frontend: Department filter tabs present (All, Sales, Customer Service, HR, Mentor, Operations, Finance)
Frontend: 6 SLA rule cards displayed with data-testid='sla-rule-{id}'
Frontend: Rule cards show name, department badge, applies_to badge, active/inactive status
Frontend: Rule cards show escalation level pills (L1, L2, L3 with time thresholds)
Frontend: Clicking rule card expands to show Escalation Levels table
Frontend: Escalation table shows Level, Name, Threshold, Action, In-App, Email, Notify Roles columns
Frontend: Sales tab filter shows 3 rules
Frontend: HR tab filter shows 2 rules
Frontend: 'New SLA Rule' button exists with data-testid='create-sla-btn'
Frontend: Create modal opens with all form fields (SLA Name, Department, Description, Applies To, Trigger Condition)
Frontend: Create modal has Escalation Levels section with Add Level button
Frontend: Each level has: Level Name, Time Threshold (hours), Action dropdown, In-App toggle, Email toggle, Notify Roles selection
Frontend: Toggle switch (data-testid='sla-toggle-{id}') activates/deactivates rules
Frontend: Toggle shows toast notification (e.g., 'CS Activation Call activated/deactivated')
Frontend: Delete button opens confirmation dialog
Frontend: Delete dialog shows rule name and 'This cannot be undone' warning
Frontend: Delete dialog has Cancel and Delete buttons
Frontend: Navigation sidebar shows 'SLA Management' link under Operations

sla_management_page: Timer icon and 'SLA Management' title displayed
Subtitle: 'Configure service level agreements across all departments'
'New SLA Rule' button in top right
stats_cards: Total Rules card with shield icon
department_filter_tabs: All, Sales, Customer Service, HR, Mentor, Operations, Finance tabs
Clicking tab filters displayed rules by department
rule_cards: Each rule has name, department badge, applies_to badge
Active status shown as green 'Active' badge or red 'Inactive' badge
Escalation level pills show L1/L2/L3 with action and time threshold
Bell and Mail icons indicate notification preferences
Toggle switch, Edit (pencil), Delete (trash), Expand (chevron) buttons
expanded_rule_view: Clicking rule card expands to show detailed escalation table
Table columns: Level, Name, Threshold, Action, In-App, Email, Notify Roles
create_edit_modal: Escalation Levels section with Add Level button
Each level: name, time_threshold_hours, action dropdown, in-app toggle, email toggle, notify_roles selection
Cancel and Create Rule/Update Rule buttons
delete_confirmation: Dialog shows rule name being deleted
seeded_rules: New Lead First Contact (Sales) - 2 levels: Warning@1hr, SLA Breach@1hr
Inactive Lead Warning (Sales) - 3 levels: Warning Mail@7days, Notify TL@10days, Reassign to Pool@13days
Pipeline Stale Lead (Sales) - 2 levels: Warning Mail@20days, Escalate to TL & COO@20days
CS Activation Call (Customer Service) - 1 level: SLA Breach@15min
HR Leave Approval (HR) - 2 levels: Reminder@1day, Escalate@2days

critical_code_review_comments: All components have proper data-testid attributes for testing
Toast notifications work correctly for toggle actions
Multi-level escalation builder UI is intuitive with Add/Remove level functionality
Notify Roles selection uses clickable pills with clear visual feedback
Stats cards update dynamically when rules are filtered
seed_data_creation: 6 default SLA rules seeded by backend on startup
context_for_next_testing_agent: SLA Management feature fully functional. 6 seeded rules: 3 Sales, 1 CS, 2 HR. Page at /sla-management. Key data-testids: sla-management-page, create-sla-btn, sla-name, sla-department, sla-description, sla-rule-{id}, sla-toggle-{id}. Backend endpoints: GET/POST /api/sla/rules, GET ?department=X filter, PUT/PATCH/DELETE /api/sla/rules/{id}. Toggle PATCH endpoint at /api/sla/rules/{id}/toggle. Login uses 'access_token' not 'token'. Navigation: SLA Management link is in Operations section of sidebar.
testing_method: Backend pytest (20 tests) + Playwright browser automation for frontend verification
